title: requests — domain events
description: Seven lifecycle events published by this module. Each is a SHIM on top of a matching event from workflow/approvals or access/grants — the shim exists so consumers of THIS module's semantics don't need to know the upstream engines' event schemas. Every event carries `ShouldDispatchAfterCommit` so listeners never react to a rolled-back state change.

events.name: AccessRequestSubmitted
events.when: Immediately after `workflow/approvals` creates the approval_instance in response to POST /access-requests. Wraps `workflow/approvals::ApprovalRequested` filtered to `action_key='access.grants.create'`.

events.name: AccessRequestApproved
events.when: Fires when the underlying approval_instance transitions to `approved` — all requirements satisfied. Wraps `workflow/approvals::ApprovalGranted` filtered to `action_key='access.grants.create'`. Does NOT itself materialise the grant — that happens on `ApprovalExecuted` in AccessRequestGrantMaterializer. This event fires FIRST (before the grant exists) so the requester can be told 'approved!' while the materialiser is still running — the notification body optimistically says 'you should now be able to access X'.

events.name: AccessRequestRejected
events.when: Fires when the underlying approval_instance transitions to `rejected` — any requirement failed. Wraps `workflow/approvals::ApprovalRejected` filtered to `action_key='access.grants.create'`. Terminal.

events.name: AccessRequestExpired
events.when: Fires when the underlying approval_instance transitions to `expired` because no decision arrived before `expires_at`. Wraps `workflow/approvals::ApprovalExpired` filtered to `action_key='access.grants.create'`. Behaviour depends on the matched template's `on_timeout`: `expire` (default) fires this event; `approve` fires AccessRequestApproved instead; `escalate` fires AccessRequestEscalated (deferred to Wave 2).

events.name: AccessRequestCancelled
events.when: Fires when the requester withdraws via POST /access-requests/{id}/withdraw. Wraps `workflow/approvals::ApprovalCancelled`. Terminal.

events.name: AccessRequestApproverNotified
events.when: Fires once per approver after `NotifyApproversJob` delivers `AccessRequestSubmittedNotification`. Separate event so analytics can measure notification delivery latency + drop rate.

events.name: AccessRequestGrantIssued
events.when: Fires from AccessRequestGrantMaterializer AFTER `access/grants::GrantIssuer::issue()` returns successfully. Distinct from AccessRequestApproved (which fires the moment the instance is approved). This event guarantees the grant row exists — downstream consumers who need the grant id (e.g. audit correlation) subscribe here, not to Approved.

notes: These events are the PUBLIC interface. Consumers of this module (billing, sports, teams, ...) subscribe to these — they NEVER subscribe to workflow/approvals events directly, because those fire for every approvable action across the platform (not just access requests).

Filtering happens at the SHIM listener boundary — `AccessRequestEventShimListener` subscribes to every workflow/approvals lifecycle event, filters to `action_key='access.grants.create'`, and rebroadcasts as the matching AccessRequest event. This keeps upstream events pure + downstream events focused.
Every event extends the abstract `AccessRequestEvent` base carrying the shared `accessRequest`, `approvalInstance`, and `requester` fields. Payloads listed above document the extra fields per event.
